39:5:3: <font color="#CC0000">“Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.</font>

39:5:8: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.</font>

39:6:1: <font color="#CC0000">“Be careful not to perform your righteous acts before men to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.</font>
39:6:2: <font color="#CC0000">So when you give to the needy, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by men. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.</font>
39:6:3: <font color="#CC0000">But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,</font>
39:6:4: <font color="#CC0000">so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.</font>
39:6:5: <font color="#CC0000">And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites. For they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by men. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.</font>
39:6:6: <font color="#CC0000">But when you pray, go into your inner room, shut your door, and pray to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.</font>
39:6:7: <font color="#CC0000">And when you pray, do not babble on like pagans, for they think that by their many words they will be heard.</font>
39:6:8: <font color="#CC0000">Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask Him.</font>
39:6:9: <font color="#CC0000">So then, this is how you should pray:  ‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be Your name.</font>
39:6:10: <font color="#CC0000">Your kingdom come, Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.</font>
39:6:11: <font color="#CC0000">Give us this day our daily bread.</font>
39:6:12: <font color="#CC0000">And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.</font>
39:6:13: <font color="#CC0000">And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.’</font>
39:6:14: <font color="#CC0000">For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.</font>
39:6:15: <font color="#CC0000">But if you do not forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive yours.</font>
39:6:16: <font color="#CC0000">When you fast, do not be somber like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces to show men they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.</font>
39:6:17: <font color="#CC0000">But when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,</font>
39:6:18: <font color="#CC0000">so that your fasting will not be obvious to men, but only to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.</font>
39:6:19: <font color="#CC0000">Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.</font>
39:6:20: <font color="#CC0000">But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moth and rust do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal.</font>
39:6:21: <font color="#CC0000">For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.</font>
39:6:22: <font color="#CC0000">The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be full of light.</font>
39:6:23: <font color="#CC0000">But if your eyes are bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness!</font>
39:6:24: <font color="#CC0000">No one can serve two masters: Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.</font>
39:6:25: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes?</font>
39:6:26: <font color="#CC0000">Look at the birds of the air: They do not sow or reap or gather into barns—and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?</font>
39:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?</font>
39:6:28: <font color="#CC0000">And why do you worry about clothes? Consider how the lilies of the field grow: They do not labor or spin.</font>
39:6:29: <font color="#CC0000">Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these.</font>
39:6:30: <font color="#CC0000">If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?</font>
39:6:31: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’</font>
39:6:32: <font color="#CC0000">For the Gentiles strive after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.</font>
39:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things will be added unto you.</font>
39:6:34: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Today has enough trouble of its own.</font>
39:7:1: <font color="#CC0000">“Do not judge, or you will be judged.</font>

39:7:5: <font color="#CC0000">You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.</font>
39:7:6: <font color="#CC0000">Do not give dogs what is holy; do not throw your pearls before swine. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and then turn and tear you to pieces.</font>

39:7:10: <font color="#CC0000">Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?</font>

39:7:13: <font color="#CC0000">Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.</font>
39:7:14: <font color="#CC0000">But small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life, and only a few find it.</font>

39:7:18: <font color="#CC0000">A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.</font>
39:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.</font>
39:7:20: <font color="#CC0000">So then, by their fruit you will recognize them.</font>

39:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">Then I will tell them plainly, ‘I never knew you; depart from Me, you workers of lawlessness!’</font>

39:7:26: <font color="#CC0000">But everyone who hears these words of Mine and does not act on them is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.</font>
39:7:27: <font color="#CC0000">The rain fell, the torrents raged, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell—and great was its collapse!”</font>

39:10:5: These twelve Jesus sent out with the following instructions: “Do not <font color="#CC0000">go onto the road of the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans</font>.
39:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">Go rather to the lost sheep of Israel.</font>
39:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">As you go, preach this message: ‘The kingdom of heaven is near.’</font>
39:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, drive out demons. Freely you have received; freely give.</font>
39:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">Do not carry any gold or silver or copper in your belts.</font>
39:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">Take no bag for the road, or second tunic, or sandals, or staff; for the worker is worthy of his provisions.</font>
39:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">Whatever town or village you enter, find out who is worthy there and stay at his house until you move on.</font>
39:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">As you enter the home, greet its occupants.</font>
39:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">If the home is worthy, let your peace rest on it; but if it is not, let your peace return to you.</font>
39:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">And if anyone will not welcome you or heed your words, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that home or town.</font>
39:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">Truly I tell you, it will be more bearable for Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that town.</font>
39:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I am sending you out like sheep among wolves; therefore be as shrewd as snakes and as innocent as doves.</font>
39:10:17: <font color="#CC0000">But beware of men; for they will hand you over to their councils and flog you in their synagogues.</font>
39:10:18: <font color="#CC0000">On My account, you will be brought before governors and kings as witnesses to them and to the Gentiles.</font>
39:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">But when they hand you over, do not worry about how to respond or what to say. In that hour you will be given what to say.</font>
39:10:20: <font color="#CC0000">For it will not be you speaking, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.</font>
39:10:21: <font color="#CC0000">Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child; children will rise against their parents and have them put to death.</font>
39:10:22: <font color="#CC0000">You will be hated by everyone because of My name, but the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.</font>
39:10:23: <font color="#CC0000">When they persecute you in one town, flee to the next. Truly I tell you, you will not reach all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.</font>
39:10:24: <font color="#CC0000">A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his master.</font>
39:10:25: <font color="#CC0000">It is enough for a disciple to be like his teacher, and a servant like his master. If the head of the house has been called Beelzebul, how much more the members of his household!</font>
39:10:26: <font color="#CC0000">So do not be afraid of them. For there is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.</font>
39:10:27: <font color="#CC0000">What I tell you in the dark, speak in the daylight; what is whispered in your ear, proclaim from the housetops.</font>
39:10:28: <font color="#CC0000">Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Instead, fear the One who can destroy both soul and body in hell.</font>
39:10:29: <font color="#CC0000">Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from the will of your Father.</font>
39:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered.</font>
39:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.</font>
39:10:32: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore everyone who confesses Me before men, I will also confess him before My Father in heaven.</font>
39:10:33: <font color="#CC0000">But whoever denies Me before men, I will also deny him before My Father in heaven.</font>
39:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">Do not assume that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.</font>
39:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">For I have come to turn  ‘a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.</font>
39:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">A man’s enemies will be the members of his own household.’</font>
39:10:37: <font color="#CC0000">Anyone who loves his father or mother more than Me is not worthy of Me; anyone who loves his son or daughter more than Me is not worthy of Me;</font>
39:10:38: <font color="#CC0000">and anyone who does not take up his cross and follow Me is not worthy of Me.</font>
39:10:39: <font color="#CC0000">Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.</font>
39:10:40: <font color="#CC0000">He who receives you receives Me, and he who receives Me receives the One who sent Me.</font>
39:10:41: <font color="#CC0000">Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward, and whoever receives a righteous man because he is a righteous man will receive a righteous man’s reward.</font>
39:10:42: <font color="#CC0000">And if anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is My disciple, truly I tell you, he will never lose his reward.”</font>

39:11:9: <font color="#CC0000">What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.</font>
39:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">This is the one about whom it is written:  ‘Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.’</font>
39:11:11: <font color="#CC0000">Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has risen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.</font>
39:11:12: <font color="#CC0000">From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been subject to violence, and the violent lay claim to it.</font>
39:11:13: <font color="#CC0000">For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.</font>
39:11:14: <font color="#CC0000">And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.</font>
39:11:15: <font color="#CC0000">He who has ears, let him hear.</font>
39:11:16: <font color="#CC0000">To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:</font>

39:11:21: <font color="#CC0000">“Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.</font>
39:11:22: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.</font>
39:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.</font>
39:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”</font>
39:11:25: At that time Jesus declared, “<font color="#CC0000">I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them</font> to little children.
39:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.</font>
39:11:27: <font color="#CC0000">All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.</font>
39:11:28: <font color="#CC0000">Come to Me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.</font>
39:11:29: <font color="#CC0000">Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.</font>
39:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.”</font>
39:12:3: Jesus replied, “<font color="#CC0000">Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were</font> hungry?
39:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">He entered the house of God, and he and his companions ate the consecrated bread, which was not lawful for them to eat, but only for the priests.</font>
39:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">Or haven’t you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath and yet are innocent?</font>
39:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.</font>
39:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">If only you had known the meaning of ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the innocent.</font>
39:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”</font>
39:12:11: He replied, “If one of <font color="#CC0000">you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will he not take hold of it and lift it out</font>?
39:12:12: <font color="#CC0000">How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”</font>
39:12:13: Then Jesus said to the man, “<font color="#CC0000">Stretch out your hand</font>.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored to full use, just like the other.
39:12:25: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “<font color="#CC0000">Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and every city or household divided against itself will not stand</font>.
39:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?</font>
39:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.</font>
39:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.</font>
39:12:29: <font color="#CC0000">Or again, how can anyone enter a strong man’s house and steal his possessions, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder his house.</font>
39:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.</font>
39:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.</font>
39:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the one to come.</font>
39:12:33: <font color="#CC0000">Make a tree good and its fruit will be good, or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad; for a tree is known by its fruit.</font>
39:12:34: <font color="#CC0000">You brood of vipers, how can you who are evil say anything good? For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.</font>
39:12:35: <font color="#CC0000">The good man brings good things out of his good store of treasure, and the evil man brings evil things out of his evil store of treasure.</font>
39:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.</font>
39:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">For by your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.”</font>
39:12:39: Jesus replied, “A wicked <font color="#CC0000">and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of the prophet</font> Jonah.
39:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.</font>
39:12:41: <font color="#CC0000">The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.</font>
39:12:42: <font color="#CC0000">The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.</font>
39:12:43: <font color="#CC0000">When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it.</font>
39:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’ On its return, it finds the house vacant, swept clean, and put in order.</font>
39:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there; and the final plight of that man is worse than the first. So will it be with this wicked generation.”</font>
39:12:48: But Jesus replied, “<font color="#CC0000">Who is My mother, and who are My</font> brothers?”

39:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.”</font>
39:13:3: And He told them many things in parables, saying, “<font color="#CC0000">A farmer went out to sow</font> his seed.
39:13:4: <font color="#CC0000">And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.</font>
39:13:5: <font color="#CC0000">Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.</font>
39:13:6: <font color="#CC0000">But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.</font>
39:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings.</font>
39:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.</font>
39:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">He who has ears, let him hear.”</font>
39:13:11: He replied, “The knowledge of <font color="#CC0000">the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them</font>.
39:13:12: <font color="#CC0000">Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.</font>
39:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">This is why I speak to them in parables:  ‘Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.’</font>
39:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled:  ‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.</font>
39:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">For this people’s heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.’</font>
39:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.</font>
39:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">For truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.</font>
39:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">Consider, then, the parable of the sower:</font>
39:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown along the path.</font>
39:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.</font>
39:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">But since he has no root, he remains for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.</font>
39:13:22: <font color="#CC0000">The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.</font>
39:13:23: <font color="#CC0000">But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.”</font>
39:13:24: Jesus put before them another parable: “<font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field</font>.
39:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">But while everyone was asleep, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and slipped away.</font>
39:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.</font>
39:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’</font>
39:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">‘An enemy did this,’ he replied.  So the servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and pull them up?’</font>
39:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">‘No,’ he said, ‘if you pull the weeds now, you might uproot the wheat with them.</font>
39:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat into my barn.’”</font>
39:13:31: He put before them another parable: “<font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field</font>.
39:13:32: <font color="#CC0000">Although it is the smallest of all seeds, yet it grows into the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and nest in its branches.”</font>
39:13:33: He told them still another parable: “<font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened</font>.”
39:13:37: He replied, “The One who sows <font color="#CC0000">the good seed is the Son of Man</font>.
39:13:38: <font color="#CC0000">The field is the world, and the good seed represents the sons of the kingdom. The weeds are the sons of the evil one,</font>
39:13:39: <font color="#CC0000">and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.</font>
39:13:40: <font color="#CC0000">As the weeds are collected and burned in the fire, so will it be at the end of the age.</font>
39:13:41: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of Man will send out His angels, and they will weed out of His kingdom every cause of sin and all who practice lawlessness.</font>
39:13:42: <font color="#CC0000">And they will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:13:43: <font color="#CC0000">Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father.  He who has ears, let him hear.</font>
39:13:44: <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and in his joy he went and sold all he had and bought that field.</font>
39:13:45: <font color="#CC0000">Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.</font>
39:13:46: <font color="#CC0000">When he found one very precious pearl, he went away and sold all he had and bought it.</font>
39:13:47: <font color="#CC0000">Once again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was cast into the sea and caught all kinds of fish.</font>
39:13:48: <font color="#CC0000">When it was full, the men pulled it ashore. Then they sat down and sorted the good fish into containers, but threw the bad away.</font>
39:13:49: <font color="#CC0000">So will it be at the end of the age: The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous,</font>
39:13:50: <font color="#CC0000">and throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:13:51: <font color="#CC0000">Have you understood all these things</font>?”  “Yes,” they answered.
39:13:52: Then He told them, “For this reason, <font color="#CC0000">every scribe who has been discipled in the kingdom of heaven is like a homeowner who brings out of his storeroom new treasures as well as old</font>.”

39:18:3: “Truly I tell you,” He said, “unless <font color="#CC0000">you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven</font>.
39:18:4: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore, whoever humbles himself like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.</font>

39:18:6: <font color="#CC0000">But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be drowned in the depths of the sea.</font>
39:18:7: <font color="#CC0000">Woe to the world for the causes of sin. These stumbling blocks must come, but woe to the man through whom they come!</font>
39:18:8: <font color="#CC0000">If your hand or your foot causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life crippled or lame than to have two hands and two feet and be thrown into the eternal fire.</font>
39:18:9: <font color="#CC0000">And if your eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.</font>

41:10:42: <font color="#CC0000">But only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, and it will not be taken away from her.”</font>
41:11:2: So Jesus told them, <font color="#CC0000">“When you pray, say:  ‘Father, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come.</font>
41:11:3: <font color="#CC0000">Give us each day our daily bread.</font>
41:11:4: <font color="#CC0000">And forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who sins against us. And lead us not into temptation.’”</font>
41:11:5: Then Jesus said to them, “Suppose one <font color="#CC0000">of you goes to his friend at midnight and says, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves</font> of bread,
41:11:6: <font color="#CC0000">because a friend of mine has come to me on a journey, and I have nothing to set before him.’</font>
41:11:7: <font color="#CC0000">And suppose the one inside answers, ‘Do not bother me. My door is already shut, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you anything.’</font>
41:11:8: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, even though he will not get up to provide for him because of his friendship, yet because of the man’s persistence, he will get up and give him as much as he needs.</font>
41:11:9: <font color="#CC0000">So I tell you: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.</font>
41:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.</font>
41:11:11: <font color="#CC0000">What father among you, if his son asks for a fish, will give him a snake instead?</font>
41:11:12: <font color="#CC0000">Or if he asks for an egg, will give him a scorpion?</font>
41:11:13: <font color="#CC0000">So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him!”</font>
41:11:17: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “<font color="#CC0000">Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and a house divided against a house</font> will fall.
41:11:18: <font color="#CC0000">If Satan is divided against himself, how can his kingdom stand? After all, you say that I drive out demons by Beelzebul.</font>
41:11:19: <font color="#CC0000">And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.</font>
41:11:20: <font color="#CC0000">But if I drive out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.</font>
41:11:21: <font color="#CC0000">When a strong man, fully armed, guards his house, his possessions are secure.</font>
41:11:22: <font color="#CC0000">But when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him, he takes away the armor in which the man trusted, and then he divides up his plunder.</font>
41:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.</font>
41:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it. Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’</font>
41:11:25: <font color="#CC0000">On its return, it finds the house swept clean and put in order.</font>
41:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there. And the final plight of that man is worse than the first.”</font>
41:11:28: But He replied, “Blessed <font color="#CC0000">rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it</font>.”
41:11:29: As the crowds were increasing, Jesus said, “<font color="#CC0000">This is a wicked generation. It demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of</font> Jonah.
41:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will be a sign to this generation.</font>
41:11:31: <font color="#CC0000">The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.</font>
41:11:32: <font color="#CC0000">The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.</font>
41:11:33: <font color="#CC0000">No one lights a lamp and puts it in a cellar or under a basket. Instead, he sets it on a stand, so those who enter can see the light.</font>
41:11:34: <font color="#CC0000">Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eyes are good, your whole body also is full of light. But when they are bad, your body is full of darkness.</font>
41:11:35: <font color="#CC0000">Be careful, then, that the light within you is not darkness.</font>
41:11:36: <font color="#CC0000">So if your whole body is full of light, with no part of it in darkness, you will be radiant, as though a lamp were shining on you.”</font>
41:11:39: “Now then,” said the Lord, “you <font color="#CC0000">Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness</font>.
41:11:40: <font color="#CC0000">You fools! Did not the One who made the outside make the inside as well?</font>
41:11:41: <font color="#CC0000">But give as alms the things that are within you, and you will see that everything is clean for you.</font>
41:11:42: <font color="#CC0000">Woe to you Pharisees! You pay tithes of mint, rue, and every herb, but you disregard justice and the love of God. You should have practiced the latter without neglecting the former.</font>
41:11:43: <font color="#CC0000">Woe to you Pharisees! You love the chief seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces.</font>
41:11:44: <font color="#CC0000">Woe to you! For you are like unmarked graves, which men walk over without even noticing.”</font>
41:11:46: <font color="#CC0000">“Woe to you as well, experts in the law!” He replied. “You weigh men down with heavy burdens, but you yourselves will not lift a finger to lighten their load.</font>
41:11:47: <font color="#CC0000">Woe to you! You build tombs for the prophets, but it was your fathers who killed them.</font>
41:11:48: <font color="#CC0000">So you are witnesses consenting to the deeds of your fathers: They killed the prophets, and you build their tombs.</font>
41:11:49: <font color="#CC0000">Because of this, the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send them prophets and apostles; some of them they will kill and others they will persecute.’</font>
41:11:50: <font color="#CC0000">As a result, this generation will be charged with the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the foundation of the world,</font>
41:11:51: <font color="#CC0000">from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, all of it will be charged to this generation.</font>
41:11:52: <font color="#CC0000">Woe to you experts in the law! For you have taken away the key to knowledge. You yourselves have not entered, and you have hindered those who were entering.”</font>
41:12:1: In the meantime, a crowd of many thousands had gathered, so that they were trampling one another. Jesus began to speak first to His disciples: “<font color="#CC0000">Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy</font>.
41:12:2: <font color="#CC0000">There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.</font>
41:12:3: <font color="#CC0000">What you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the daylight, and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops.</font>
41:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more.</font>
41:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear the One who, after you have been killed, has authority to throw you into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear Him!</font>
41:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God.</font>
41:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.</font>
41:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, everyone who confesses Me before men, the Son of Man will also confess him before the angels of God.</font>
41:12:9: <font color="#CC0000">But whoever denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.</font>
41:12:10: <font color="#CC0000">And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.</font>
41:12:11: <font color="#CC0000">When you are brought before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, do not worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say.</font>
41:12:12: <font color="#CC0000">For at that time the Holy Spirit will teach you what you should say.”</font>
41:12:14: But Jesus replied, “<font color="#CC0000">Man, who appointed Me judge or executor between you</font>?”
41:12:15: And He said to them, <font color="#CC0000">“Watch out! Guard yourselves against every form of greed, for one’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.</font>”
41:12:16: Then He told them a parable: “<font color="#CC0000">The ground of a certain rich man</font> produced an abundance.
41:12:17: <font color="#CC0000">So he thought to himself, ‘What shall I do, since I have nowhere to store my crops?’</font>
41:12:18: <font color="#CC0000">Then he said, ‘This is what I will do: I will tear down my barns and will build bigger ones, and there I will store up all my grain and my goods.</font>
41:12:19: <font color="#CC0000">Then I will say to myself, “You have plenty of good things laid up for many years. Take it easy. Eat, drink, and be merry!”’</font>
41:12:20: <font color="#CC0000">But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be required of you. Then who will own what you have accumulated?’</font>
41:12:21: <font color="#CC0000">This is how it will be for anyone who stores up treasure for himself but is not rich toward God.”</font>
41:12:22: Then Jesus said to His disciples, <font color="#CC0000">“Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.</font>
41:12:23: <font color="#CC0000">For life is more than food, and the body more than clothes.</font>
41:12:24: <font color="#CC0000">Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storehouse or barn; yet God feeds them. How much more valuable you are than the birds!</font>
41:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?</font>
41:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">So if you cannot do such a small thing, why do you worry about the rest?</font>
41:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">Consider how the lilies grow: They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these.</font>
41:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, how much more will He clothe you, O you of little faith!</font>
41:12:29: <font color="#CC0000">And do not be concerned about what you will eat or drink. Do not worry about it.</font>
41:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">For the Gentiles of the world strive after all these things, and your Father knows that you need them.</font>
41:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">But seek His kingdom, and these things will be added unto you.</font>
41:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father is pleased to give you the kingdom.</font>
41:12:33: <font color="#CC0000">Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide yourselves with purses that will not wear out, an inexhaustible treasure in heaven, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.</font>
41:12:34: <font color="#CC0000">For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.</font>
41:12:35: <font color="#CC0000">Be dressed for service and keep your lamps burning.</font>
41:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">Then you will be like servants waiting for their master to return from the wedding banquet, so that when he comes and knocks, they can open the door for him at once.</font>
41:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are those servants whom the master finds on watch when he returns. Truly I tell you, he will dress himself to serve and will have them recline at the table, and he himself will come and wait on them.</font>
41:12:38: <font color="#CC0000">Even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night and finds them alert, those servants will be blessed.</font>
41:12:39: <font color="#CC0000">But understand this: If the homeowner had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.</font>
41:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you do not expect.”</font>
41:12:42: And the Lord answered, “<font color="#CC0000">Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their portion</font> at the proper time?
41:12:43: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns.</font>
41:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">Truly I tell you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.</font>
41:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">But suppose that servant says in his heart, ‘My master will be a long time in coming,’ and he begins to beat the menservants and maidservants, and to eat and drink and get drunk.</font>
41:12:46: <font color="#CC0000">The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate. Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.</font>
41:12:47: <font color="#CC0000">That servant who knows his master’s will but does not get ready or follow his instructions will be beaten with many blows.</font>
41:12:48: <font color="#CC0000">But the one who unknowingly does things worthy of punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required; and from him who has been entrusted with much, even more will be demanded.</font>
41:12:49: <font color="#CC0000">I have come to ignite a fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!</font>
41:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">But I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!</font>
41:12:51: <font color="#CC0000">Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, but division.</font>
41:12:52: <font color="#CC0000">From now on, five in one household will be divided, three against two and two against three.</font>
41:12:53: <font color="#CC0000">They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.”</font>
41:12:54: Then Jesus said to the crowds, “As soon as you <font color="#CC0000">see a cloud rising in the west, you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and that is</font> what happens.
41:12:55: <font color="#CC0000">And when the south wind blows, you say, ‘It will be hot,’ and it is.</font>
41:12:56: <font color="#CC0000">You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and sky. Why don’t you know how to interpret the present time?</font>
41:12:57: <font color="#CC0000">And why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?</font>
41:12:58: <font color="#CC0000">Make every effort to reconcile with your adversary while you are on your way to the magistrate. Otherwise, he may drag you off to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and the officer may throw you into prison.</font>
41:12:59: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the very last penny.”</font>
41:13:2: To this He replied, “Do you think <font color="#CC0000">that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered</font> this fate?
41:13:3: <font color="#CC0000">No, I tell you. But unless you repent, you too will all perish.</font>
41:13:4: <font color="#CC0000">Or those eighteen who were killed when the tower of Siloam collapsed on them: Do you think that they were more sinful than all the others living in Jerusalem?</font>
41:13:5: <font color="#CC0000">No, I tell you. But unless you repent, you too will all perish.”</font>
41:13:6: Then Jesus told this parable: “<font color="#CC0000">A man had a fig tree that was planted in his vineyard. He went to look for fruit</font> on it, but did not find any.
41:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">So he said to the keeper of the vineyard, ‘Look, for the past three years I have come to search for fruit on this fig tree and haven’t found any. Therefore cut it down! Why should it use up the soil?’</font>
41:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">‘Sir,’ the man replied, ‘leave it alone again this year, until I dig around it and fertilize it.</font>
41:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">If it bears fruit next year, fine. But if not, you can cut it down.’”</font>
41:13:12: When Jesus saw her, He called her over and said, <font color="#CC0000">“Woman, you are set free from your disability.</font>”
41:13:15: “You hypocrites!” the Lord replied. “Does <font color="#CC0000">not each of you on the Sabbath untie his ox or donkey from the stall and lead it to</font> water?
41:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">Then should not this daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has kept bound for eighteen long years, be released from her bondage on the Sabbath day?”</font>
41:13:18: Then Jesus asked, “<font color="#CC0000">What is the kingdom of God like? To what can I compare it</font>?
41:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">It is like a mustard seed that a man tossed into his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the birds of the air nested in its branches.”</font>
41:13:20: Again He asked, “To what can <font color="#CC0000">I compare the kingdom of God</font>?
41:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">It is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.”</font>
41:13:24: <font color="#CC0000">“Make every effort to enter through the narrow door. For many, I tell you, will try to enter and will not be able.</font>
41:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">After the master of the house gets up and shuts the door, you will stand outside knocking and saying, ‘Lord, open the door for us.’  But he will reply, ‘I do not know where you are from.’</font>
41:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">Then you will say, ‘We ate and drank with you, and you taught in our streets.’</font>
41:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">And he will answer, ‘I tell you, I do not know where you are from. Depart from me, all you evildoers.’</font>
41:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but you yourselves are thrown out.</font>
41:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">People will come from east and west and north and south, and will recline at the table in the kingdom of God.</font>
41:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">And indeed, some who are last will be first, and some who are first will be last.”</font>
41:13:32: But Jesus replied, “<font color="#CC0000">Go tell that fox, ‘Look, I will keep driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I</font> will reach My goal.’
41:13:33: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless, I must keep going today and tomorrow and the next day, for it is not admissible for a prophet to perish outside of Jerusalem.</font>
41:13:34: <font color="#CC0000">O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her, how often I have longed to gather your children together as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were unwilling!</font>
41:13:35: <font color="#CC0000">Look, your house is left to you desolate. And I tell you that you will not see Me again until you say, ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord.’”</font>
41:14:3: So Jesus asked the experts in the law and the Pharisees, “<font color="#CC0000">Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath</font> or not?”
41:14:5: And He asked them, “<font color="#CC0000">Which of you whose son or ox falls into a pit on the Sabbath day</font> will not immediately pull him out?”
41:14:8: <font color="#CC0000">“When you are invited to a wedding banquet, do not sit in the place of honor, in case someone more distinguished than you has been invited.</font>
41:14:9: <font color="#CC0000">Then the host who invited both of you will come and tell you, ‘Give this man your seat.’ And in humiliation, you will have to take the last place.</font>
41:14:10: <font color="#CC0000">But when you are invited, go and sit in the last place, so that your host will come and tell you, ‘Friend, move up to a better place.’ Then you will be honored in front of everyone at the table with you.</font>
41:14:11: <font color="#CC0000">For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”</font>
41:14:12: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited Him, <font color="#CC0000">“When you host a dinner or a banquet, do not invite your friends or brothers or relatives or rich neighbors. Otherwise, they may invite you in return, and you will be repaid.</font>
41:14:13: <font color="#CC0000">But when you host a banquet, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, and the blind,</font>
41:14:14: <font color="#CC0000">and you will be blessed. Since they cannot repay you, you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.”</font>
41:14:16: But Jesus replied, “<font color="#CC0000">A certain man prepared a great banquet and invited many</font> guests.
41:14:17: <font color="#CC0000">When it was time for the banquet, he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited, ‘Come, for everything is now ready.’</font>
41:14:18: <font color="#CC0000">But one after another they all began to make excuses. The first one said, ‘I have bought a field, and I need to go see it. Please excuse me.’</font>
41:14:19: <font color="#CC0000">Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to try them out. Please excuse me.’</font>
41:14:20: <font color="#CC0000">Still another said, ‘I have married a wife, so I cannot come.’</font>
41:14:21: <font color="#CC0000">The servant returned and reported all this to his master. Then the owner of the house became angry and said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the city, and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.’</font>
41:14:22: <font color="#CC0000">‘Sir,’ the servant replied, ‘what you ordered has been done, and there is still room.’</font>
41:14:23: <font color="#CC0000">So the master told his servant, ‘Go out to the highways and hedges and compel them to come in, so that my house will be full.</font>
41:14:24: <font color="#CC0000">For I tell you, not one of those men who were invited will taste my banquet.’”</font>
41:14:26: <font color="#CC0000">“If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters—yes, even his own life—he cannot be My disciple.</font>
41:14:27: <font color="#CC0000">And whoever does not carry his cross and follow Me cannot be My disciple.</font>
41:14:28: <font color="#CC0000">Which of you, wishing to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost to see if he has the resources to complete it?</font>
41:14:29: <font color="#CC0000">Otherwise, if he lays the foundation and is unable to finish the work, everyone who sees it will ridicule him,</font>
41:14:30: <font color="#CC0000">saying, ‘This man could not finish what he started to build.’</font>
41:14:31: <font color="#CC0000">Or what king on his way to war with another king will not first sit down and consider whether he can engage with ten thousand men the one coming against him with twenty thousand?</font>
41:14:32: <font color="#CC0000">And if he is unable, he will send a delegation while the other king is still far off, to ask for terms of peace.</font>
41:14:33: <font color="#CC0000">In the same way, any one of you who does not give up everything he has cannot be My disciple.</font>
41:14:34: <font color="#CC0000">Salt is good, but if the salt loses its savor, with what will it be seasoned?</font>
41:14:35: <font color="#CC0000">It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile, and it is thrown out.  He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”</font>
41:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">“What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the pasture and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?</font>
41:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders,</font>
41:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">comes home, and calls together his friends and neighbors to tell them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost sheep!’</font>
41:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">In the same way, I tell you that there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous ones who do not need to repent.</font>
41:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">Or what woman who has ten silver coins and loses one of them does not light a lamp, sweep her house, and search carefully until she finds it?</font>
41:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">And when she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors to say, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost coin.’</font>
41:15:10: <font color="#CC0000">In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of God’s angels over one sinner who repents.”</font>
41:15:11: Then Jesus said, “There was <font color="#CC0000">a man who had two sons</font>.
41:15:12: <font color="#CC0000">The younger son said to him, ‘Father, give me my share of the estate.’ So he divided his property between them.</font>
41:15:13: <font color="#CC0000">After a few days, the younger son got everything together and journeyed to a distant country, where he squandered his wealth in wild living.</font>
41:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">After he had spent all he had, a severe famine swept through that country, and he began to be in need.</font>
41:15:15: <font color="#CC0000">So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.</font>
41:15:16: <font color="#CC0000">He longed to fill his belly with the pods the pigs were eating, but no one would give him a thing.</font>
41:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">Finally he came to his senses and said, ‘How many of my father’s hired servants have plenty of food? But here I am, starving to death!</font>
41:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">I will get up and go back to my father and say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.</font>
41:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Make me like one of your hired servants.”’</font>
41:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">So he got up and went to his father. But while he was still in the distance, his father saw him and was filled with compassion. He ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.</font>
41:15:21: <font color="#CC0000">The son declared, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’</font>
41:15:22: <font color="#CC0000">But the father said to his servants, ‘Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.</font>
41:15:23: <font color="#CC0000">Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let us feast and celebrate.</font>
41:15:24: <font color="#CC0000">For this son of mine was dead and is alive again! He was lost and is found!’ So they began to celebrate.</font>
41:15:25: <font color="#CC0000">Meanwhile the older son was in the field, and as he approached the house, he heard music and dancing.</font>
41:15:26: <font color="#CC0000">So he called one of the servants and asked what was going on.</font>
41:15:27: <font color="#CC0000">‘Your brother has returned,’ he said, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has him back safe and sound.’</font>
41:15:28: <font color="#CC0000">The older son became angry and refused to go in. So his father came out and pleaded with him.</font>
41:15:29: <font color="#CC0000">But he answered his father, ‘Look, all these years I have served you and never disobeyed a commandment of yours. Yet you never gave me even a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.</font>

41:15:31: <font color="#CC0000">‘Son, you are always with me,’ the father said, ‘and all that is mine is yours.</font>
41:15:32: <font color="#CC0000">But it was fitting to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’”</font>
41:16:1: Jesus also said to His disciples, <font color="#CC0000">“There was a rich man whose manager was accused of wasting his possessions.</font>
41:16:2: <font color="#CC0000">So he called him in to ask, ‘What is this I hear about you? Turn in an account of your management, for you cannot be manager any longer.’</font>
41:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">The manager said to himself, ‘What shall I do, now that my master is taking away my position? I am too weak to dig and too ashamed to beg.</font>
41:16:4: <font color="#CC0000">I know what I will do, so that after my removal from management, people will welcome me into their homes.’</font>
41:16:5: <font color="#CC0000">And he called in each one of his master’s debtors. ‘How much do you owe my master?’ he asked the first.</font>
41:16:6: <font color="#CC0000">‘A hundred measures of olive oil,’ he answered.  ‘Take your bill,’ said the manager. ‘Sit down quickly, and write fifty.’</font>
41:16:7: <font color="#CC0000">Then he asked another, ‘And how much do you owe?’  ‘A hundred measures of wheat,’ he replied.  ‘Take your bill and write eighty,’ he told him.</font>
41:16:8: <font color="#CC0000">The master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly. For the sons of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own kind than are the sons of light.</font>
41:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves, so that when it is gone, they will welcome you into eternal dwellings.</font>
41:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much.</font>
41:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">So if you have not been faithful with worldly wealth, who will entrust you with true riches?</font>
41:16:12: <font color="#CC0000">And if you have not been faithful with the belongings of another, who will give you belongings of your own?</font>
41:16:13: <font color="#CC0000">No servant can serve two masters. Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.”</font>
41:16:15: So He said to them, “You <font color="#CC0000">are the ones who justify yourselves before men, but God knows your hearts. For what is prized among men is detestable before God</font>.
41:16:16: <font color="#CC0000">The Law and the Prophets were proclaimed until John. Since that time, the gospel of the kingdom of God is being preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.</font>
41:16:17: <font color="#CC0000">But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for a single stroke of a pen to drop out of the Law.</font>
41:16:18: <font color="#CC0000">Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.</font>
41:16:19: <font color="#CC0000">Now there was a rich man dressed in purple and fine linen, who lived each day in joyous splendor.</font>
41:16:20: <font color="#CC0000">And a beggar named Lazarus lay at his gate, covered with sores</font>
41:16:21: <font color="#CC0000">and longing to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Even the dogs came and licked his sores.</font>
41:16:22: <font color="#CC0000">One day the beggar died and was carried by the angels to Abraham’s side. And the rich man also died and was buried.</font>
41:16:23: <font color="#CC0000">In Hades, where he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham from afar, with Lazarus by his side.</font>
41:16:24: <font color="#CC0000">So he cried out, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue. For I am in agony in this fire.’</font>
41:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">But Abraham answered, ‘Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, while Lazarus received bad things. But now he is comforted here, while you are in agony.</font>
41:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">And besides all this, a great chasm has been fixed between us and you, so that even those who wish cannot cross from here to you, nor can anyone cross from there to us.’</font>
41:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">‘Then I beg you, father,’ he said, ‘send Lazarus to my father’s house,</font>
41:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">for I have five brothers. Let him warn them, so that they will not also end up in this place of torment.’</font>
41:16:29: <font color="#CC0000">But Abraham replied, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; let your brothers listen to them.’</font>
41:16:30: <font color="#CC0000">‘No, father Abraham,’ he said, ‘but if someone is sent to them from the dead, they will repent.’</font>
41:16:31: <font color="#CC0000">Then Abraham said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.’”</font>
41:17:1: Jesus said to His disciples, “<font color="#CC0000">It is inevitable that stumbling blocks will come, but woe to the one through whom they come</font>!

41:17:3: <font color="#CC0000">Watch yourselves. If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.</font>
41:17:4: <font color="#CC0000">Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to say, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”</font>

41:17:18: <font color="#CC0000">Was no one found except this foreigner to return and give glory to God?”</font>

41:17:20: When asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, Jesus replied, “<font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of God will not come with</font> observable signs.
41:17:21: <font color="#CC0000">Nor will people say, ‘Look, here it is,’ or ‘There it is.’ For you see, the kingdom of God is in your midst.”</font>
41:17:22: Then He said to the disciples, “<font color="#CC0000">The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it</font>.
41:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">People will tell you, ‘Look, there He is!’ or ‘Look, here He is!’ Do not go out or chase after them.</font>
41:17:24: <font color="#CC0000">For just as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so will be the Son of Man in His day.</font>
41:17:25: <font color="#CC0000">But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.</font>
41:17:26: <font color="#CC0000">Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man:</font>
41:17:27: <font color="#CC0000">People were eating and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.</font>
41:17:28: <font color="#CC0000">It was the same in the days of Lot: People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.</font>
41:17:29: <font color="#CC0000">But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.</font>
41:17:30: <font color="#CC0000">It will be just like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed.</font>
41:17:31: <font color="#CC0000">On that day, let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve his possessions. Likewise, let no one in the field return for anything he has left behind.</font>
41:17:33: <font color="#CC0000">Whoever tries to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.</font>
41:17:34: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed: one will be taken and the other left.</font>
41:17:35: <font color="#CC0000">Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.”</font>
41:17:37: <font color="#CC0000">“Where, Lord?” they asked.  Jesus answered, “Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.”</font>
41:18:2: “In a certain town <font color="#CC0000">there was a judge who neither feared God</font> nor respected men.
41:18:3: <font color="#CC0000">And there was a widow in that town who kept appealing to him, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.’</font>
41:18:4: <font color="#CC0000">For a while he refused, but later he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God nor respect men,</font>
41:18:5: <font color="#CC0000">yet because this widow keeps pestering me, I will give her justice. Then she will stop wearing me out with her perpetual requests.’”</font>
41:18:6: And the Lord said, “Listen to the words of <font color="#CC0000">the unjust judge</font>.
41:18:7: <font color="#CC0000">Will not God bring about justice for His elect who cry out to Him day and night? Will He continue to defer their help?</font>
41:18:8: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, He will promptly carry out justice on their behalf. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He find faith on earth?”</font>
41:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">“Two men went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.</font>
41:18:11: <font color="#CC0000">The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed, ‘God, I thank You that I am not like the other men—swindlers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.</font>
41:18:12: <font color="#CC0000">I fast twice a week and pay tithes of all that I acquire.’</font>
41:18:13: <font color="#CC0000">But the tax collector stood at a distance, unwilling even to lift up his eyes to heaven. Instead, he beat his breast and said, ‘God, have mercy on me, a sinner!’</font>
41:18:14: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, this man, rather than the Pharisee, went home justified. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”</font>
41:18:16: But Jesus called the children to Him and said, “Let the <font color="#CC0000">little children come to Me, and do not hinder them! For the kingdom of God</font> belongs to such as these.
41:18:17: <font color="#CC0000">Truly I tell you, anyone who does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.”</font>
41:18:19: “<font color="#CC0000">Why do you call Me good?” Jesus replied. “No one is good except God</font> alone.
41:18:20: <font color="#CC0000">You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your father and mother.’”</font>
41:18:22: On hearing this, Jesus told him, “You still lack <font color="#CC0000">one thing: Sell everything you own and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow Me</font>.”
41:18:24: Seeing the man’s sadness, Jesus said, “<font color="#CC0000">How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God</font>!
41:18:25: <font color="#CC0000">Indeed, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”</font>
41:18:27: But Jesus said, “What is <font color="#CC0000">impossible with man is possible with God</font>.”
41:18:29: “Truly <font color="#CC0000">I tell you,” Jesus replied, “no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of</font> God
41:18:30: <font color="#CC0000">will fail to receive many times more in this age—and in the age to come, eternal life.”</font>
41:18:31: Then Jesus took the Twelve aside and said to them, “Look, <font color="#CC0000">we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything the prophets have written about the Son of Man will be</font> fulfilled.

41:18:33: <font color="#CC0000">They will flog Him and kill Him, and on the third day He will rise again.”</font>
41:18:41: <font color="#CC0000">“What do you want Me to do for you?”  “Lord,” he said, “let me see again.”</font>
41:18:42: <font color="#CC0000">“Receive your sight!” Jesus replied. “Your faith has healed you.</font>”

41:19:15: <font color="#CC0000">When he returned from procuring his kingship, he summoned the servants to whom he had given the money, to find out what each one had earned.</font>
41:19:16: <font color="#CC0000">The first servant came forward and said, ‘Master, your mina has produced ten more minas.’</font>
41:19:17: <font color="#CC0000">His master replied, ‘Well done, good servant! Because you have been faithful in a very small matter, you shall have authority over ten cities.’</font>
41:19:18: <font color="#CC0000">The second servant came and said, ‘Master, your mina has made five minas.’</font>
41:19:19: <font color="#CC0000">And to this one he said, ‘You shall have authority over five cities.’</font>
41:19:20: <font color="#CC0000">Then another servant came and said, ‘Master, here is your mina, which I have laid away in a piece of cloth.</font>
41:19:21: <font color="#CC0000">For I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man. You withdraw what you did not deposit and reap what you did not sow.’</font>
41:19:22: <font color="#CC0000">His master replied, ‘You wicked servant, I will judge you by your own words. So you knew that I am a harsh man, withdrawing what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?</font>
41:19:23: <font color="#CC0000">Why then did you not deposit my money in the bank, and upon my return I could have collected it with interest?’</font>
41:19:24: <font color="#CC0000">Then he told those standing by, ‘Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has ten minas.’</font>
41:19:25: <font color="#CC0000">‘Master,’ they said, ‘he already has ten!’</font>
41:19:26: <font color="#CC0000">He replied, ‘I tell you that everyone who has will be given more; but the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.</font>

41:20:11: <font color="#CC0000">So he sent another servant, but they beat him and treated him shamefully, sending him away empty-handed.</font>
41:20:12: <font color="#CC0000">Then he sent a third, but they wounded him and threw him out.</font>
41:20:13: <font color="#CC0000">‘What shall I do?’ asked the owner of the vineyard. ‘I will send my beloved son. Perhaps they will respect him.’</font>
41:20:14: <font color="#CC0000">But when the tenants saw the son, they discussed it among themselves and said, ‘This is the heir. Let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’</font>
41:20:15: <font color="#CC0000">So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.  What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?</font>
41:20:16: <font color="#CC0000">He will come and kill those tenants, and will give the vineyard to others</font>.”  And when the people heard this, they said, “May such a thing never happen!”

41:20:34: Jesus answered, “<font color="#CC0000">The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage</font>.

41:21:11: <font color="#CC0000">There will be great earthquakes, famines, and pestilences in various places, along with fearful sights and great signs from heaven.</font>
41:21:12: <font color="#CC0000">But before all this, they will seize you and persecute you. On account of My name they will deliver you to the synagogues and prisons, and they will bring you before kings and governors.</font>

He was a murderer from the beginning, refusing to uphold the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, because he is a liar and the father of lies.</font>

42:10:1: <font color="#CC0000">“Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever does not enter the sheepfold by the gate, but climbs in some other way, is a thief and a robber.</font>

42:10:4: <font color="#CC0000">When he has brought out all his own, he goes on ahead of them, and his sheep follow him because they know his voice.</font>
42:10:5: <font color="#CC0000">But they will never follow a stranger; in fact, they will flee from him because they do not recognize his voice.”</font>
42:10:7: So He said to them again, “Truly, truly, <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep</font>.
42:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">All who came before Me were thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.</font>
42:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">I am the gate. If anyone enters through Me, he will be saved. He will come in and go out and find pasture.</font>
42:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I have come that they may have life, and have it in all its fullness.</font>
42:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.</font>
42:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">The hired hand is not the shepherd, and the sheep are not his own. When he sees the wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and runs away. Then the wolf pounces on them and scatters the flock.</font>
42:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">The man runs away because he is a hired servant and is unconcerned for the sheep.</font>
42:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">I am the good shepherd. I know My sheep and My sheep know Me,</font>
42:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">just as the Father knows Me and I know the Father. And I lay down My life for the sheep.</font>

42:10:18: <font color="#CC0000">No one takes it from Me, but I lay it down of My own accord. I have authority to lay it down and authority to take it up again. This charge I have received from My Father.”</font>
42:10:25: “<font color="#CC0000">I already told you,” Jesus replied, “but you did not believe. The works I do in My Father’s name</font> testify on My behalf.
42:10:26: <font color="#CC0000">But because you are not My sheep, you refuse to believe.</font>
42:10:27: <font color="#CC0000">My sheep listen to My voice; I know them, and they follow Me.</font>
42:10:28: <font color="#CC0000">I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one can snatch them out of My hand.</font>
42:10:29: <font color="#CC0000">My Father who has given them to Me is greater than all. No one can snatch them out of My Father’s hand.</font>

42:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">If he called them gods to whom the word of God came—and the Scripture cannot be broken—</font>

42:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">Jesus replied, “You do not realize now what I am doing, but later you will understand.”</font>

42:14:1: <font color="#CC0000">“Do not let your hearts be troubled. You believe in God; believe in Me as well.</font>

42:14:10: <font color="#CC0000">Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me? The words I say to you, I do not speak on My own. Instead, it is the Father dwelling in Me, performing His works.</font>
